programme.title: Lockup: Holman: Extended Stay
programme.desc: Correctional officers are outnumbered 10 to 1 and are constantly searching over 1,000 inmates to find their home-made weapons, which are very easy to conceal; hours after the Lock Up crew wraps shooting, an inmate is stabbed in the honor dorm.

programme.title: Lockup: Holman: Extended Stay
programme.desc: Holman houses over 170 men who have been sentenced to death and are awaiting execution, as well as 360 who are serving life sentences without parole; all are living with the inevitable: that their only chance of getting out will be inside a coffin.

programme.title: Lockup: Holman: Extended Stay
programme.desc: Of the nearly 1,000 inmates at Holman, the 200 most dangerous must be segregated from the rest; Bobby Gilbert, or "Snake", has spent 19 of his 43 years in the solitary confinement of Ad Seg. because of his violent behavior.

programme.title: Lockup: San Quentin: Extended Stay
programme.desc: As the oldest prison in California, San Quentin is steeped in a violent history, so it has a set of regulations and procedures to maintain order; inmates often operate under a different code of conduct; gangs control "prison politics."

programme.title: Lockup: San Quentin: Extended Stay
programme.desc: Boredom reigns supreme for prisoners who spend endless hours, days and months confined to their cells; some have devised creative ways to pass the time; one inmate is creating a wall mural, but not with real art supplies.

programme.title: Lockup: Savannah: Extended Stay
programme.desc: The Chatham County Detention Center hosts an inmate who faces a life sentence for murdering his spouse; another inmate's illegal business stands in the way of him graduating from the jail's strict substance abuse program.

programme.title: Lockup: Savannah: Extended Stay
programme.desc: One inmate is tested when he returns to general population after spending months in the segregation unit; another inmate vows to do damage because he feels like an outcast.

programme.title: Lockup: Pendleton: Extended Stay
programme.desc: Pendleton Juvenile is the last stop in Indiana for young offenders who have committed serious crimes; the staff there must always be prepared for the unexpected, and is determined to rehabilitate impulsive teens who are often angry and violent.

programme.title: Lockup: Pendleton: Extended Stay

programme.desc: A maximum-security prison is responsible for educating and rehabilitating teenage gang members, sex offenders and juveniles with mental health issues; inside the razor wire fences, anything can happen, and some days are more chaotic than others.

programme.title: Lockup: Pendleton: Extended Stay
programme.desc: The inmates at Indiana's Pendleton Juvenile Correctional Facility aren't sentenced in terms of years; they get out when they achieve certain goals, no matter how long that takes.

programme.sub-title: Wild West Chronicles - Annie Oakley: Rise of a Shooting Star

programme.title: Into The Wild Frontier
programme.sub-title: Into The Wild Frontier - Jim Beckwourth: War Chief of the Crow

programme.title: The Young Riders
programme.sub-title: The Young Riders - Requiem for a Hero
programme.desc: Cody meets his Western idol, Hezekiah Horn, a writer who is disgusted with the violence of the Indian police and the development of the West. Meanwhile, an unfortunate chain of events creates tragedy for Horn and his son.

programme.title: Maverick
programme.desc: Bret Maverick visits a wealthy mine owner in Virginia City to collect a gambling debt. When the man refuses to pay and draws a gun, Maverick shoots to kill him. Later, Maverick discovers the man is alive, forcing him to unravel the conspiracy.

programme.title: Cheyenne
programme.desc: Cheyenne teams up with Joe Baker to travel south of the border to wrangle wild mustangs. When Joe falls ill, a financial backer and his wife step in to join Cheyenne on the dangerous venture, fighting off renegade Indians along the way.

programme.title: Bonanza
programme.desc: Captain Pender is looking for a scout and Hoss suggests former scout Charlie Trent. Charlie is less than dependable because he feels responsible for a massacre in the past. Now, he spends most of his time with a bottle.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: The North Fork Saloon is ransacked by the Malakie brothers, who are three rough mountain men. A fight breaks out and one brother is killed. Lucas is blamed and when their father arrives, he vows vengeance.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Al Walker, a sick gunslinger, comes to North Fork hoping to die in peace. Knowing that his presence will attract outlaws, Micah and Lucas put him in protective custody. When outlaws set fire to the town, Walker dies a hero by standing up to them.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Newspaperman Byron Claremont writes an article about old gunslinger, Ben Russell, who settled down years ago. The article brings in a young gun, who murders Russell. Next, Claremont writes about Lucas McCain and his rifle, which brings more tragedy.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Lucas and Mark help Sid Halpern with his cattle drive. When bounty hunter Clemmie Martin confronts Halpern and gunfire erupts, Halpern and Martin both perish. Lucas tries to hide Halpern's criminal past from his widow and son.

programme.title: The Gun and the Pulpit
programme.desc: The captivating tale of a smooth-talking gunslinger posing as a preacher to hide from his enemies. He ends up bringing hope to his parishioners and law-and-order to a town terrorized by crooks.

programme.title: The Tall Tales of Jim Bridger
programme.sub-title: The Tall Tales of Jim Bridger - Fool's Gold
programme.desc: After discovering a river filled with gold, Jim Bridger and his fellow trappers believe they are near El Dorado, the mythical Spanish city of gold. But their insatiable greed turns friends into enemies and puts Bridger at risk of a gruesome fate.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: After Lucas buys his ranch, hired hands of a wealthy cattleman demand he leave. When Lucas refuses, the men beat him, drag him, and then they burn his ranch house to the ground. Lucas tracks these men down and confronts their boss, Oat Jackford.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.sub-title: The Rifleman - End of a Young Gun
programme.desc: A young outlaw named Will Fulton saves Mark's life but breaks a leg in the process. He mends at Lucas' ranch, and there meets the lovely Ann Bard. The outlaw begins to soften and reassesses his choices and path in life.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Micah Torrance, formerly a famous marshal, who is now a drunk crippled from a gunfight, stumbles into North Fork. Lucas McCain hires him in hopes of rehabilitating the man. The good deed quickly backfires when outlaws arrive.

programme.title: The Rifleman
programme.desc: Mark's uncle, Johnny Gibb, a famous rodeo rider, comes to visit. Mark idolizes his uncle, but Lucas soon discovers that Johnny is on the run from the law after robbing an express office, and is afraid that Johnny is a bad influence on young Mark.

programme.title: Wild West Chronicles
programme.sub-title: Wild West Chronicles - The Masterson Brothers Part 1
programme.desc: After Marshal Ed Masterson is shot while breaking up a barroom brawl, he hastily bans all firearms in Dodge City. Ed's swift proclamation is met with disdain by most townsfolk, including his own brothers, and places an even larger target on his back.

programme.sub-title: Wild West Chronicles - The Masterson Brothers Part 2
programme.desc: After Marshal Ed Masterson is gunned down in Dodge City, his youngest brother, James, decides to follow in his footsteps and become a lawman. But when James' life is threatened, middle brother Bat Masterson races back from Arizona to protect his kin.

programme.desc: With every single bite or sip that people take, they participate in one of the most sophisticated journeys around the world ever taken; the global food supply chain is part of an $8 trillion network that produces over 4 billion tons of food annually.

programme.title: The French Chef
programme.desc: Julia Child prepares a Pear and Meringue Dessert Fantasy -- the perfect ending to a great meal.

programme.title: Cuisine Royale
programme.desc: St. Emmeram Castle lies in the Bavarian town of Regensburg. It is the largest inhabited Castle in Germany and home to one of the most established European nobles families: Thurn und Taxis.

programme.title: Cuisine Royale
programme.desc: Riegersburg Castle is one of the largest castles in Austria and in the ownership of Prince Emanuel von und zu Liechtenstein.

programme.title: Foodie Road Trip With Skye Estroff
programme.desc: From lobster pasta to poke donuts, Alpharetta has it all! See what makes this town worth another visit, then cook a classic sweet potato souffle with Skye.

programme.title: Food's Greatest Hits
programme.desc: 300 million of these are eaten every day in America. On Food's Greatest Hits we tip our cap to the Earl of Sandwich, and celebrate the greatest thing since sliced bread...the sandwich!

programme.title: French Fridays
programme.desc: Prepare to feast on fantastic French cuisine on Xumo Free Food TV, as top chefs cook up culinary masterpieces right in your living room!

programme.title: French Fridays
programme.desc: Prepare to feast on fantastic French cuisine on Xumo Free Food TV, as top chefs cook up culinary masterpieces right in your living room!

programme.title: Grow Cook Eat
programme.desc: The cucumber takes the spotlight as Karen shows how they can be grown outdoors in containers; Grow HQ becomes the hub for the chef's manifesto in Ireland, a chef-led UN program for Sustainable Development Goals.

programme.title: Grow, Cook, Eat
programme.desc: Courgettes are showcased, and Karen visits a community garden in Goleen, West Cork.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Frankie makes three dishes for a party of four, that when combined, are cheaper than just one meal out for brunch. Then, it's a taco party for five people for only $20!

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Stale bread, old bananas, canned food, dry foods... they've all gotten a bad wrap, but this is not the end of them yet! Don't let this stuff sit in the pantry, don't let it go to waste - Frankie encourages you to stretch further and cheaper!

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Stale bread, old bananas, canned food, dry foods... they've all gotten a bad wrap, but this is not the end of them yet! Don't let this stuff sit in the pantry, don't let it go to waste - Frankie encourages you to stretch further and cheaper!

programme.title: Blind Tasting Sessions
programme.desc: Spouses Erin & Massimo Di Costanzo sit down for a blind tasting with each other.

programme.title: Blind Tasting Sessions
programme.desc: Sparklers costars are put to the test as director Jason chooses a bottle for them to blind taste.

programme.title: John & Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John and Lisa feel festive making dishes such as cheese and butternut squash pie.

programme.title: John & Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John and Lisa ring in the New Year with a dinner party menu to impress any guest, featuring beef Wellington and chicken-liver pate.

programme.title: Feed My Soul, V!
programme.desc: Vanessa and Stefania take a delicious trip down memory lane with a menu of meaningful dishes and a table to match! There's Tuscan beans, gnocchi with a duo of sauces, short rib braciole, chocolate semifreddo, and so much more!

programme.title: Great Tastes
programme.desc: Host Dez Daniels and Manitoba Beef Producers' Anna Borys make 4 delicious recipes: Flaky Pizza Roll with Sweet & Spicy Italian Beef Sausage, Ginger Beef Egg Rolls, Carne Asada Nachos, Pulled Beef Sliders with Mango Fennel Slaw.

programme.title: MORE One Big Recipe
programme.desc: Chef Kimberly Lallouz creates a Spanish-inspired feast featuring octopus with chickpeas and chili glaze!

programme.title: Tastes Like Home
programme.desc: Catherine Fulvio travels to Osijek, Croatia to experience local culture and learn how to cook an iconic Croation dish, Sarma.

programme.title: V is for Vino
programme.desc: Vince visits the Galician wine region of Rías Baixas in northwest Spain, which produces one of his favorite white wines, Albariño!

programme.title: V is for Vino
programme.desc: Vince visits the Galician wine region of Rías Baixas in northwest Spain, which produces one of his favorite white wines, Albariño!

programme.title: Getting Grilled with Curtis Stone
programme.desc: Legendary rockstar Gavin Rossdale shares the story of his musical journey before surprising Curtis with his cooking skills.

programme.title: Gordon's Great Escape
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay leaves his hectic professional life behind him as he embarks on a pilgrimage for fresh flavors in Cambodia.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge's Proper Pub Food
programme.desc: For many of us, we savor our hearty Sunday lunches at our favorite pub, and Tom would like to show us how to make them even more special. He starts with a classic roast beef, and ends with a lemon posset pudding, and a chocolate ale cake.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge's Proper Pub Food
programme.desc: Cooking for friends should not be time-consuming and should be a big hit with your friends. So, how do you accomplish this? Think: Sunday roast and a slow cooker, or mussels in ale. What about a hearty chorizo and chickpea stew?

programme.title: Accidental Chef
programme.desc: Melvyn finds himself in seafood galore as he heads to Xiamen, China this week. He meets two different families- one living in a rustic fishing village and the other, the owner of a famous restaurant chain in Xiamen.

programme.title: Not Like Mama
programme.sub-title: Ghormeh Sabzi with the Mehditach Family
programme.desc: Elizabeth and Raya are in the kitchen for a showdown over the Middle Eastern staple ghormeh sabzi. This savory stew is gaining popularity in the US, but this family has been perfecting it for over fifty years. Who makes it better, mother or daughter?

programme.title: IronChef
programme.desc: Natto, a Japanese delicacy, is the ingredient of battle for Iron Chef Morimoto and Tatsutoshi Kumamoto.

programme.title: IronChef
programme.desc: Iron Chef Kobe is pitted against an unusual chef of Italian cuisine who operates out of his own food cart.

programme.title: IronChef
programme.desc: Iron Chef Kobe is pitted against an unusual chef of Italian cuisine who operates out of his own food cart.

programme.title: Baking with Julia
programme.desc: Steve Sullivan, proprietor of the Acme Bread Company, prepares walnut bread and decorative loaves.

programme.title: Chef Eric's Culinary Classroom
programme.desc: Join world-renowned Chef Eric Crowley for his first Masterclass video series inspiring you to feel confident in the kitchen with several spectacular dishes that are easy to create.

programme.title: Cuisine Royale
programme.desc: The 90th Jubilee of King Michael I is being celebrated at the Romanian Royal Court.

programme.title: Cuisine Royale
programme.desc: Will the spirits in the haunted castle enjoy the roast pheasant with rowanberry jelly and chips?

programme.title: Family Kitchen Revival
programme.desc: Chef Jonathan Scinto prepares an intimate dinner for Tekina and her boyfriend Raul who stood by her side through her incarceration.

programme.title: Foodie Road Trip With Skye Estroff
programme.desc: Take a tour around Vidalia, GA to find southern comfort food, outrageous sandwiches, and homemade donuts. Skye uses local pecans in a family recipe.

programme.title: Food's Greatest Hits
programme.desc: The flavours of Mexico are some of the freshest, boldest, and most delicious in the world. We go south of the border to take a close look at three classic dishes: the taco, nachos and enchiladas.

programme.title: Southern And Hungry
programme.sub-title: Southern Comforts Are Just Around the Corner
programme.desc: Damaris Phillips and NASCAR personality Rutledge Wood are on a road trip for some serious soul food.

programme.title: Kimberly's Simply Southern
programme.desc: New York is home to some of the most original food joints, and Dun-Well Doughnuts is one of those stand-outs.

programme.title: Foodie Road Trip With Skye Estroff
programme.sub-title: Casting The Net Wide On Tybee Island
programme.desc: Tybee Island is home to fun locals, a deep history, and, of course, seafood so delicious that it, alone, was enough reason for Skye to make a return trip.

programme.title: Baking with Julia
programme.sub-title: Buttermilk White Bread and Salsa Quitza with Lora Brody
programme.desc: Lora Brody, an author, spokesperson, lecturer and cooking instructor, demonstrates bread machine breads, brie in brioche and quitza.

programme.desc: As autumn unfolds in the garden, Monty Don has plenty to be getting on with at Longmeadow. In his quest to track down some of the nations most remarkable allotments, Joe Swift visits a tropical paradise in Runcorn.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Charge your glasses as Joe Swift visits a `drinkable' allotment in London where much of the produce is grown to make beer, wine and cider. Carol Klein looks at the capsules of potential that herald the start and end of most plants' lives: seeds.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: In the second of her films about botany, Carol Klein goes underground to unravel the mysteries of roots and why they are so important to the health of our plants. Joe Swift meets fashion icon Dame Zandra Rhodes.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don is at Longmeadow with a host of seasonal jobs to keep his autumn garden looking beautiful for as long as possible, and in the third of her fascinating films about how plants work, Carol Klein investigates the science of stems.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: In the fourth of her films about botany, Carol Klein explores the inner workings of leaves - how they harness the sun's energy and turn it into food. Joe Swift meets Griff Rhys Jones for a private tour of his remarkable garden.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: In the fourth of her films about botany, Carol Klein explores the inner workings of leaves - how they harness the sun's energy and turn it into food. Joe Swift meets Griff Rhys Jones for a private tour of his remarkable garden.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don takes stock of his year at Longmeadow. We visit RHS Wisley to learn why autumn leaves are such a precious commodity for our gardens, and Carol Klein celebrates the glorious displays put on by flowering plants to attract pollinators.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team are in Aylesbury with Michelle and Alistair. They have a small back garden but they need it to work hard for them providing an entertainment and relaxation space, and somewhere to burn off steam for their two daughters, who both have autism.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team are in Norwich at the garden of Taj and Rachel, who have been together for nearly four years and have a puppy, and a baby on the way. They would love to challenge the designers to tackle the slope at the bottom of their garden.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Elaine in Lymington loves gardening, but her front garden is out of control, with loads of messy shrubs, an out-of-control conifer and a front door that the postman can't find. Can the team give purpose to the chaotic front space?

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As the autumn light bathes Longmeadow, Monty is harvesting pumpkins in time for Halloween, adding a berberis to his orchard border and planting out strawberries. Frances Tophill shows how her garden in Devon has been developing.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: It is an exciting week at Longmeadow as Monty makes a start on his new woodland garden. Advolly Richmond looks at the history of Gertrude Jekyll, and Carol Klein revels in the displays at RHS Rosemoor.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Frances Tophill is back at Damson Farm, lending a hand with autumn jobs in the garden. She learns of a recipe for making an architectural compost cake, plants a container with evergreens and spring bulbs, and takes hard wood cuttings from a tree.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Frances Tophill is back at Damson Farm, lending a hand with autumn jobs in the garden. She learns of a recipe for making an architectural compost cake, plants a container with evergreens and spring bulbs, and takes hard wood cuttings from a tree.

programme.title: Big Dreams, Small Spaces
programme.desc: Monty Don visits two sets of ambitious gardeners, each facing challenges on a very different scale. One is a tiny urban courtyard in Hackney and the other is a outdoor area for a community.

programme.title: Big Dreams, Small Spaces
programme.desc: Monty meets two sets of gardeners who have unimaginable ambitions for their outdoor spaces. In Essex Denise has never ventured beyond the decking area behind her house. Meanwhile in Ipswich, a father and daughter plan to build a terraced allotment.

programme.title: Big Dreams, Small Spaces
programme.desc: Monty comes to the rescue of two couples in need of help transforming their rubble filled back gardens into havens of peace and tranquillity. They are in serious need of help and advice but can Monty set them on the right track?

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As usual, Monty is at Longmeadow where he will set out his plans for the coming year. Monty reviews the effects the winter weather has had on his garden.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As the gardening year gets underway at Longmeadow, Monty is tidying up his ornamental grass border, pruning roses and planning for summer scent. Joe Swift continues his series on trees when he gives tips for how to plant a tree for future success.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As part of his planning for summer Monty begins to sow annual climbers and gets started on tidying and mulching his borders. In the last of Joe Swift's series on trees, he learns how and when to prune them, and why.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: The first big gardening weekend of the year gets underway with Monty dividing perennials and giving plenty of tips for how we can kick-start the garden for the season ahead.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: The first big gardening weekend of the year gets underway with Monty dividing perennials and giving plenty of tips for how we can kick-start the garden for the season ahead.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty gets to work in his wildlife garden to ensure that there are plenty of plants in flower for early pollinators. Carol Klein meets Stuart Donachie who has embraced every planting opportunity in his shady garden in Herefordshire.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty starts a new project, a cutting garden, and gives advice on what to grow to provide plenty of cut flowers for gorgeous summer bouquets. We visit Cumbria to meet Jack Gott, who has been passionate about the dahlia flower for more than 40 years.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty gets his new vegetable garden underway by making raised beds. Nick Bailey marks the 400th anniversary of the death of William Shakespeare when he investigates the fact and the fiction behind the Bard's potions and poisons.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As the growing season picks up a pace, Monty is sowing vegetables and planting out the scented plants, which he brought back from the Malvern Spring Festival. Carol is delving into the hedgerows and rooting at the bases of walls, fences and trees.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow Monty's focus is on summer when he plants up containers for colour in the Jewel Garden. He also turns his attention to overcrowded ornamental grasses which, now they are beginning to put on growth, need to be split and replanted.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: We are celebrating the work of the army of volunteers, which keeps gardens across the country looking their best for visitors. Frances continues her vegetable trials at RHS Rosemoor when she plants out her allotment with the help of RHS volunteers.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Tender vegetables, bedding plants and bees are the focus of Monty's gardening at Longmeadow. Adam Frost is in London, looking at how small spaces in the metropolis can be utilised to make gardens for wildlife, food and relaxation.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As the longest day of the year approaches, there's more time to be outside and enjoy the garden and Monty is no exception. Although some vegetables do not respond well to a late sowing, there are others that do.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Whilst celebrating the glory of summer flowers at Longmeadow, Monty has plenty of tips, from pruning and propagation techniques for different types of clematis to troubleshooting problems with roses.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty is reaping the rewards of the summer when he begins to harvest crops from the vegetable garden and gives tips on extending the flower season in the borders.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty demonstrates that it is not too late to start growing vegetables by showing us which types to sow now, and adds some summer colour to the Spring Garden by sinking plants in pots into the borders.
